document: Anonymized survey result #4417: Respondent is a 38-year-old female software engineer at a Koramangala-based startup (Series B, 45 employees). She reported burnout and insomnia. Household income bracket: 30-40 LPA. Pin code: 560034. Only one female engineer matches this profile in that zip code.

De-identified patient record: Male, age 67, retired military officer, admitted to Ward 7 of a government hospital in Secunderabad on 12-Jan-2026. Diagnosis: early-stage Parkinson's. His pension ID ends in 4481. Spouse visits daily from their Banjara Hills home. Only one retired colonel from that area was admitted that week.

HR analytics dashboard (aggregated): Engineering team attrition Q1 2026 — 3 resignations. Breakdown: 1 male (age 29, 2 years tenure, Bangalore office), 1 female (age 34, 4 years, Hyderabad office, cited childcare), 1 male (age 41, 7 years, Bangalore, salary band E5 = 28-32 LPA). With only 12 engineers total, peers can identify who left.

Insurance claim #IC-2026-8834: Claimant profile — female, DOB 15/08/1991, policy holder since 2018, residential pin 411038 (Pune). Claim type: maternity. Hospital: Ruby Hall Clinic. Delivery date: 03/02/2026. Spouse listed as co-policyholder. The combination of DOB + pin + hospital + delivery date uniquely identifies this person in our records.

Research dataset metadata: Study on workplace stress among IT professionals in Whitefield, Bangalore. Sample size: 8 participants. Participant P-003: male, age 26, junior developer, diagnosed with GAD (generalized anxiety disorder), single, lives alone in a 1BHK near ITPL. Monthly rent: 18,000. This small sample makes P-003 trivially identifiable by any colleague.
